headers.comment: Only the Trunk-hashed bundle is immutable -- its filename changes every build, so a year-long cache is safe and desirable. Everything else lives at a stable path and must revalidate. Scoping immutability to this prefix (rather than a **/*.js|css|wasm glob) keeps it from ever catching sw.js, /js/*, or app.css, which is what pinned stale code on installed iOS PWAs.

Stable-path assets that change between builds: the service worker, the hand-authored audio JS, the stylesheet, and the HTML shell. no-cache means the browser may hold a copy but must revalidate before using it, so an edit reaches an installed PWA on the next load.
